task_completion.description: Configure Stellarium to the correct location (44.00 S, 170.47 E) and time (March 15, 2024, 09:00 UTC).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium location window, date/time window, or the bottom status bar shows the location near 44.00 S, 170.47 E (Aoraki Mackenzie) and the date/time as 2024-03-15 09:00 UTC.

task_completion.description: Disable the atmosphere and ground, and enable constellation lines and labels in Stellarium.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium sky view shows a black background (no blue atmosphere), no landscape/ground at the bottom, and constellation lines and names are clearly visible.

task_completion.description: Capture screenshots of Crux, the Large Magellanic Cloud, and Alpha Centauri to ~/Pictures/stellarium/.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or image viewer shows at least 3 screenshot files in the ~/Pictures/stellarium/ directory, and the images display the requested celestial objects.

task_completion.description: Create a tour briefing document at ~/Desktop/tour_briefing.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open editing 'tour_briefing.txt', or the file 'tour_briefing.txt' is visible on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Include the tour date and location in the briefing document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'tour_briefing.txt' explicitly mention the date (March 15, 2024) and the location (Aoraki Mackenzie or 44.00 S, 170.47 E).

task_completion.description: Include a brief note for Crux, the Large Magellanic Cloud, and Alpha Centauri explaining their significance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'tour_briefing.txt' contain specific paragraphs or bullet points for Crux, the Large Magellanic Cloud, and Alpha Centauri, with text describing their astronomical or cultural significance.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Stellarium application to configure the view and take screenshots.
integrity.visual_evidence: Stellarium's distinct user interface (toolbars, menus, status bar) is visible in the trajectory, rather than the agent just downloading images from a web browser.

integrity.description: The captured screenshots are genuine outputs from the configured Stellarium instance.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots in ~/Pictures/stellarium/ match the requested display settings (no ground, no atmosphere, constellation lines/labels enabled) and Stellarium's rendering style.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor to create the briefing document.
integrity.visual_evidence: A GUI text editor (like gedit, mousepad) or an interactive terminal editor (like nano, vim) is visible on screen with the typed content, rather than the file being generated via a hidden script or single echo command.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Location: 44.00 S, 170.47 E. Target Date/Time: 2024-03-15 09:00:00 UTC. Display Settings: Atmosphere OFF, Landscape OFF, Constellation Lines ON, Constellation Labels ON. Required Screenshots: At least 3 images in ~/Pictures/stellarium/ showing Crux, Large Magellanic Cloud, and Alpha Centauri. Required Document: ~/Desktop/tour_briefing.txt containing the date, location, and notes on the 3 objects.
